q: According to Newton's second law, an object that is accelerating must have a ___ force acting on it.
This force is known as the ___ force.
It has been shown in the previous chapter that for uniform circular motion (speed $v = \text{constant}$), the acceleration is ___ acceleration.
In the absence of this force, the object would not move along a circle but move in a ___ line.
Substituting $v = r\omega$ in Eq. (2.1), $F_c = ___$.
For an object to move in a circle, a force is needed to ___ from its straight line path.
The ___ in the string provides the required centripetal force for the ball to keep moving in circular path.
Some examples of the centripetal force are the gravitational force of the earth on the ___ orbiting the earth.
There are different forces which can provide centripetal force depending on the motion of ___ under various conditions.
When whirling a body attached to a string, a tension force is ___.
An ___ force exists between the sun and the earth in a direction perpendicular to the path of the earth.
The ___ attractive force between the nucleus and the electron acts as the required centripetal force.
When a car turns in a circular path or a curve, a ___ force exists between the road and the car tires.
The horizontal component of ___ force is the required centripetal force.
The upward thrust (lifting force) acts normally to an ___ body.
When the aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component acts normally to the motion of the aeroplane, causing the aeroplane to turn in a ___.
On a flat road (unbanked road), this force is supplied by ___ between the tires and the road.
If the frictional force is not sufficient, such as under wet and slippery conditions, the car will ___ out of the circular path.
Banking of roads is raising the ___ edge of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
The angle that the inclined track makes with horizontal is called angle of ___.
The normal force $F_N$ exerted by a banked road is ___ to the road.
For every banked curve, there is a ___ speed at which the entire centripetal force is supplied by the horizontal component of the normal force.
Since there is no vertical motion, the net force on car in y direction is ___.
Centripetal force is the force acting towards the ___ of the circular path around which the object is moving.
According to Newton's third law the ball exerts a ___ force on the hand.
However, when the car moves around a curve the person feels an ___ force exerted on him.
Actually, the centrifugal force is not a real ___ force.
It is an apparent or fictitious force which is equal and ___ to the centripetal force.
A centrifuge is a piece of equipment that puts an object in ___ around a fixed axis.
In a laboratory centrifuge that uses sample test tubes, the radial acceleration causes denser particles to settle to the ___ of the tube.
Centrifugal pump is a hydraulic machine which converts mechanical energy into ___ energy.
The rotating ___ moves water or other fluids by using centrifugal force.
The centrifugal action of a washing machine is used in ___ drying the clothes.
The centrifugal governor is a device used to measure and regulate the ___ of a machine.
The centrifugal governors consist of two balls of equal ___, which are attached to the arms.
As the engine is working, these balls also revolve around the ___ at a certain speed.
Centripetal force is the force acting on an object in circular motion which is directed towards the axis of ___.
Centrifugal force in a circular motion is not a ___ force.
Centrifugal force acts radially away from the centre of the circle. It results from the effect of ___.
If a car is travelling through a curve on a circular horizontal road, the centripetal force provided by the force of ___.
When a car in motion takes a sudden turn in a curved path, passengers in the car experience an ___ push.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the ___ force.
Removing the excess water by spin drying clothes in a washing machine is with the help of ___ force.
An athlete weighing $790\text{ N}$ is running a curve at a speed of $6\text{ m s}^{-1}$ in an arc. The radius of curvature of arc is ___ m.
The mass of the athlete is calculated as $m = \frac{w}{g} = \frac{790}{9.8} = $ ___ kg.
The required centripetal force to run a curve is calculated as $F_c = \frac{mv^2}{r} = \frac{80.61(6)^2}{5} = $ ___ N.
Frictional force between the feet and the ground provides the ___ force.
If the radius of curvature is smaller, the centripetal force will become ___.
He will slip since the frictional force is ___ than the required centripetal force.
When a skater forms a circle, he has to lean ___.
